discovery.run_summary: Five mid-cap names reporting May 26-28. All earnings 8-Ks fell back to primary (cover documents, no Exhibit 99.1 financials), severely limiting quantitative read. Best directional read is MOD: filings disclose a pending Reverse Morris Trust spin/merger with Gentherm that materially derisks the print's framing as a focused data-center cooling pure-play; ASAN shows recent C-suite churn (COO, GC, CEO at peers) and Atlas-style slowing not directly applicable. MDB has a fresh CEO transition (Nov 2025) and CAO swap (Feb 2026) entering its first-full-quarter-under-new-CEO print, which adds risk.

discoveries.name: Modine Manufacturing
discoveries.filings_evidence: FY25 10-K (filed May 2025) shows Data Center Cooling grew from 12% to 25% of net sales YoY with management calling it 'exceptionally strong growth' and expecting continued strong growth in FY26 driven by AI/HPC heat loads; segment mix is shifting hard toward the highest-margin vertical. The Q3 FY26 10-Q (Feb 2026) added a new risk factor disclosing a pending Reverse Morris Trust spin of Performance Technologies to merge with Gentherm — this leaves RemainCo concentrated in data center cooling and commercial HVAC/refrigeration, the segments that have been the growth engine. FY25 operating income rose $42M YoY on the Climate Solutions strength.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Earnings 8-Ks all fell back to cover documents so specific guide-vs-actual is not readable from this bundle; 10-K narrative reads as measured/cautious on PT verticals (agriculture, commercial vehicle weakness explicitly called out) while consistently bullish on Data Center Cooling.
discoveries.setup: pre-earnings read on Q4 FY26 (year-end March 31, 2026) print with pending RMT transaction reframing the story
discoveries.thesis: Filings show a company whose mix has decisively shifted to the AI-data-center-cooling vertical (25% of sales and growing), with management announcing a transaction that will divest the weak Performance Technologies drag. The market may still be valuing MOD on the legacy mixed-industrial multiple while the filings show a clear path to a focused data-center cooling pure-play. Year-end print is the last one before the spin reframing takes hold.
discoveries.what_confirms: Climate Solutions segment revenue growth at or above the prior cadence, Data Center Cooling disclosed as a higher % of total sales, constructive language on Gentherm transaction timing/synergies, and ongoing weakness in PT (which is being divested) being framed as a transient issue.
discoveries.what_kills: Data Center Cooling growth materially decelerating, hyperscaler order pushouts, RMT transaction delay or termination, or Climate Solutions margin compression from capacity-expansion costs outpacing revenue.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a mid-cap industrial announces a spin of its weaker segment between two prints, the print just before the spin closes often gets re-rated on the RemainCo mix — track whether the data-center-cooling % of sales narrative dominates the call.
research_pointers: Get the actual Q3 FY26 (Dec 2025) press release financials to baseline Data Center Cooling growth rate going into Q4
Check sell-side consensus for whether models reflect pre- or post-spin segment economics
Listen for RMT timing commentary and any Gentherm shareholder vote update on the call
Watch for hyperscaler customer concentration disclosure (two unnamed global tech customers were called out as significant in FY25 10-K)

discoveries.name: MongoDB
discoveries.filings_evidence: Q3 FY26 10-Q (Dec 2025) explicitly states 'the adverse macroeconomic conditions resulted in slower than historical growth of our existing Atlas applications for the nine months ended October 31, 2025' — this is an unusually direct acknowledgment of Atlas consumption deceleration in the risk factors. The Q3 8-K (Nov 3, 2025) disclosed CEO Dev Ittycheria's resignation effective Nov 9, 2025, replaced by CJ Desai from Cloudflare. The Q4 8-K (March 2, 2026) discloses a further C-suite change — President of Field Operations Cedric Pech resignation effective April 15, 2026, and a new CAO joining April 14, 2026. Net ARR expansion rate of 121% in FY26 Q4 (per 10-K) is down from prior cyclical highs.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Earnings 8-Ks all fell back to cover documents — specific historical guide-vs-actual cadence not readable from this bundle; however 10-Q language flagging Atlas slowdown is itself a tell that management is managing expectations down.
discoveries.setup: First reporting quarter (Q1 FY27) under new CEO CJ Desai with concurrent departure of head of field sales — classic kitchen-sink setup
discoveries.thesis: The combination of (a) explicit Atlas consumption slowdown language in the most recent 10-Q, (b) a brand-new CEO in his first full reporting quarter (high incentive to reset the bar), and (c) the sales-leader resignation announced 6 weeks before the print suggests elevated risk that the Q1 print and/or the FY27 guide come in below what the market currently embeds. Even if the headline beats, a new-CEO 'reset' on out-quarters is a recognized pattern.
discoveries.what_confirms: FY27 revenue guide below sell-side consensus, Atlas growth rate continuing to decelerate vs. the 9-month commentary, lowered net ARR expansion rate below 121%, or commentary from CJ Desai about 'reprioritizing' or 'rebuilding the sales motion.'
discoveries.what_kills: AI workload wins drive an Atlas reacceleration, new CEO chooses to hold the line on guide rather than reset, or strong Voyage AI-related contract wins materially offset macro softness.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a software CEO transition coincides with a sales-leader departure inside 60 days of the first print, the new CEO almost always resets the out-quarter bar — track whether MDB confirms this pattern.
research_pointers: Read the actual Q4 FY26 (March 2, 2026) press release for the FY27 initial guide and Atlas revenue growth rate
Check whether CJ Desai pre-announced any strategy/restructuring at investor events between Nov 2025 and now
Watch for any commentary on AI-application customer wins (Voyage AI integration) being material vs. promotional
Cross-check sell-side ARR expansion forecasts against the 121% FY26 exit

skipped.reason: All four earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents (no Exhibit 99.1 financials readable); 10-K shows backlog growth $861M→$1.06B and stable 104% NRR but without quarterly revenue/guide cadence the directional read is too thin — RATIONAL/UNCLEAR.

All earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents; Q10-Q risk factors note 'no material changes' from 10-K. With no quarterly revenue, ARR, or Elastic Cloud growth numbers readable, no basis for a directional pre-print call — UNCLEAR.

All earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents. The Dec 2, 2025 8-K does disclose simultaneous COO and GC resignations effective Dec 31, 2025 (negative signal), and the Mar 2, 2026 8-K shows a $160M buyback increase (offsetting positive signal). 10-K confirms NRR stuck at 97% Core / 96% over-100K, both still under 100% which is structurally weak. Mixed signals plus no readable financial cadence — UNCLEAR/RATIONAL.

discovery.no_signals_note: All five candidates' earnings 8-Ks shipped as cover-document fallbacks rather than Exhibit 99.1 press releases, which substantially degrades the quality of the quarterly-cadence read. Calls were made primarily on the 10-K business narrative and 10-Q risk-factor changes; confidence is held at 3 across both directional flags due to this input-quality constraint.
